You can migrate a specific schema(user/user's tables) by "iqunload" utility with "-ul" option.
You may find an example regarding your question from KBA 3544548 below.

entry.title: SY-UNAME is different from login user
entry.content:
hi Team,

We are debugging an OData service, in that we have a method UserProfileSet(), we dont pass any input to this and basically it gives us the logged In user details as output. Please find the below URL for the same. 

/sap/opu/odata/sap/Z********TR_SRV/userProfileSet('')?$format=json 

Earlier it was working fine , by providing the details of the logged In User, but now for any login either was**sts or c*h-fiori1 or any other user s*r_ad***mn we are getting an output  as 1069****81 & relevant details of  1069****81. Please help us and do the needful. 

The code to fetch the user Details at Source code is as shown below. 

 

 

 

  METHOD userprofileset_get_entity.

    DATA:

      lt_return      TYPE bapirettab,

      lw_return      TYPE bapiret2,

      lv_propertyref TYPE string.

    DATA:lw_user_address TYPE bapiaddr3.

    DATA:lv_approver TYPE boolean.

    DATA et_bapiret TYPE bapirettab.

    DATA lw_zprdprofile TYPE zprdprofile.

    CALL METHOD zcl_prt_str_requirements=>get_user_details

      EXPORTING

        iv_userid       = sy-uname

      IMPORTING

        ew_user_address = lw_user_address.

    lw_zprdprofile-fullname = lw_user_address-firstname.

    lw_zprdprofile-uname = sy-uname.

    SELECT SINGLE * FROM zprdprofile INTO lw_zprdprofile WHERE uname = sy-uname.

    IF sy-subrc = 0 .

      er_entity = CORRESPONDING #( lw_zprdprofile ).

      er_entity-iscreater = abap_true.

      CALL METHOD zcl_prt_str_requirements=>pending_approval_list

        EXPORTING

          iv_is_approver = abap_true

        IMPORTING

          ev_is_approver = lv_approver

          et_bapiret     = et_bapiret.

      er_entity-isapprover = lv_approver.

    ELSE.

      CALL METHOD zcl_prt_str_requirements=>pending_approval_list

        EXPORTING

          iv_is_approver = abap_true

        IMPORTING

          ev_is_approver = lv_approver

          et_bapiret     = et_bapiret.

      IF lv_approver IS INITIAL.

        lw_return-id = 'Z***_EWM'.

        lw_return-log_msg_no = '001'.

        lw_return-type = 'I'.

        MESSAGE ID 'Z***_EWM' TYPE 'I' NUMBER 001

                INTO lw_return-message .

        APPEND lw_return TO lt_return.

        er_entity = CORRESPONDING #( lw_zprdprofile ).

      ELSE.

        er_entity = CORRESPONDING #( lw_zprdprofile ).

        er_entity-isapprover = lv_approver.

      ENDIF.

    ENDIF.

    lv_propertyref = 'userProfile'.

    me->report_header_messages(

      EXPORTING

        it_bapiret2    =  lt_return                " Table with BAPI Return Information

        iv_propertyref = lv_propertyref

        iv_save        =      abap_true            " Single-Character Flag

*        iv_str_num     =                  " S** Number

    ).

    no_cache( ).

  ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: Camera Access issue for Intune-Distributed users on iOS devices for SAP Mobile Start
entry.content:
Hi

We have an issue whereby users cannot gain access to the camera when they are onboarded on to SAP Mobile Start via Intune. In this case the video button in the top navigation bar displays in red, and the camera feature is unavailable - because the Intune-released users on iOS devices have not granted explicit permission to the camera. When users are onboarded via the QR code there is a specific pop up which requests access to the Camera so the issue is only raised via the Intune onboarding process. 

Looking for some assistance with the following points:

  1. Verify if there are any specific settings or configuration adjustments required for Intune-released users who have not completed the QR code-based onboarding process.
  2. Provide guidance on how to enable camera access for these users or any steps they need to follow for granting camera permissions on their iOS devices.
  3. If there are any system-wide configurations in SAP Mobile Services that need to be adjusted for these users, please advise on the best approach.
  4. Confirm if there in any feature flag I can enable to access the camera for users who have not given permission.

Any assistance would be appreciated.

Kind Regards

Phil Cooley

entry.title: AI’s Growth Paradox: Why More Data and Power Aren’t Enough
entry.content:
Imagine a time when every new data point added to an AI model led to exponential improvements—when each additional layer of computing power made models more intelligent, insightful, and transformative. But now, it feels like we are reaching a turning point. Just like trying to quench a thirst with too much water, adding more data and computational resources to AI seems to be yielding diminishing returns. The idea of endlessly improving AI capabilities is now being challenged, and it might be time to rethink our strategies.

The economic principle of diminishing marginal utility teaches us that the satisfaction gained from consuming more of a good decreases as we consume more of it. Strangely enough, this concept may help us understand the current landscape of AI development, particularly with discussions around OpenAI's Orion and Google's latest AI models. Are we approaching a plateau in AI's capabilities, where more data and computing power yield less and less progress? This blog explores both sides of the argument, outlining the reasons for agreement and disagreement while embedding a storyline that is both intriguing and informative.

Part 1: The Stalled Ascent of AI

Recent news about OpenAI's Orion and Google's generative models highlights the challenges faced by AI's top players: despite using massive amounts of data and computational power, the expected improvements in areas like contextual understanding, nuanced conversation, and general problem-solving capabilities have not materialized as anticipated. This has led to the perception that progress in AI intelligence is incremental, if not stalled. Some argue that the once-popular logic—"add more data and more computing power to get better results"—is inherently flawed. Let’s dig deeper into the reasons supporting this viewpoint.

Data Limitations: In the past, the race was all about accumulating vast amounts of data. However, we have reached a point where most of the available data on the internet has already been used in training these models. Even synthetic data—data created by AI—isn't resulting in significant leaps in capability anymore. For instance, areas like conversational nuance and deeper context understanding still face challenges due to the limitations of current training data, leading to stagnation.

Computing Power Bottlenecks: The issue of computing power is twofold—it comes down to GPU chips and energy consumption. Currently, data centers require immense energy, and many tech companies are scrambling to secure deals for renewable energy sources to support their operations. For example, Microsoft has signed multiple deals to expand their use of renewable energy to sustain their AI development centers. Additionally, there's a dependency on a limited number of chip manufacturers, leading to a bottleneck in GPU availability. Sam Altman even hinted at rationing chips for OpenAI's frontier models, which would result in less focus on other developments. This scarcity of computing resources could significantly slow the progress of AI.

Algorithmic Constraints: The reliance on specific training techniques such as the "attention mechanism" has its limits. Attention-based models are notorious for their hunger for both data and computational power. The pursuit of alternative methods has led to the development of ChatGPT 10, a model that enhances reasoning capabilities with the same set of data but requires higher inference time. Additionally, newer approaches like the TTT model are promising increased efficiency with reduced energy needs. For example, TTT claims to achieve comparable results while using significantly less power, making it a potentially transformative innovation. With billions being poured into frontier models, further breakthroughs seem almost inevitable, but for now, the progress feels sluggish.

Part 2: The Tip of the Iceberg

 

So, have we reached the limit of what AI can do? I believe that’s a shortsighted view. Even if we assume for a moment that we are at a tipping point—that future innovations will bring diminishing returns—the current capabilities of AI are still so powerful that their potential will take years to fully unlock. We're merely at the tip of an iceberg that promises massive business value and societal transformation.

Recent findings, such as the Enterprise State of IT 2024 report by Menlo Park, reflect this sentiment. AI spending has surged from $2.3 billion in 2023 to $13.8 billion this year, a clear signal that enterprises are shifting from experimentation to execution. A visual representation of this spending increase could help illustrate the rapid adoption of AI. The budget split is also revealing: while 60% of AI investment comes from innovation budgets, the remaining 40% is coming from more permanent allocations, indicating a growing commitment to long-term AI transformation.

The implication here is profound: we’re transitioning into an era where AI isn't just a fancy, experimental tool—it's becoming a cornerstone of business strategies. For example, Amazon has successfully integrated AI into its core operations, using AI-driven systems to optimize its supply chain, forecast demand, and streamline logistics, which has led to significant operational efficiency and cost savings. AI adoption is still in its early stages, and the coming years will be about maximizing the transformation it can bring, especially through innovative AI agents. For example, Amazon has been integrating AI agents to optimize their supply chain and logistics, making a significant impact on operational efficiency.

Part 3: What Lies Ahead?

 

To conclude, here's my take on where we're headed:

  1. Algorithmic Innovation: The future will see the emergence of more efficient algorithms that require less computational power and data to achieve better performance. We’re already seeing glimpses of this through models like ChatGPT 10 and TTT, and I believe we will see more breakthroughs that make AI both powerful and sustainable. For instance, ongoing research by DeepMind on energy-efficient learning could pave the way for more accessible AI.

  2. Business Use Cases and AI Agents: One of the keys to unlocking AI's full potential lies in how it is applied to real-world problems. AI agents, in particular, have the capacity to drastically change the way businesses operate, streamlining processes and revealing new opportunities for value creation. Imagine an AI agent that not only automates customer service but also provides proactive insights to improve customer satisfaction—such advancements could revolutionize industries from healthcare to logistics, and we’re only beginning to scratch the surface.

  3. Transformative Value: Even if we have hit a "plateau," the current innovations will continue to generate massive value for businesses for the next decade. This is not the end, but rather a period of refining, adapting, and finding novel applications for what we have already built. For instance, the financial sector is seeing significant AI-driven transformations, from fraud detection to personalized banking experiences.

The Law of Diminishing Returns... Or Not?

 

In economics, diminishing returns might signal an end to expansion, but when it comes to AI, we are merely shifting gears. It’s not that progress is impossible; it’s just that we’re entering a new phase—one that demands smarter approaches, efficient algorithms, and innovative applications to truly harness the vast potential that lies within AI. The road ahead may be steeper, but the rewards at the top are more promising than ever.

To all those leveraging AI, now is the time to think creatively about how these tools can transform your business. Whether it's through new algorithms, efficient energy use, or novel AI agents, the potential is immense, and the journey is just beginning.

The blog was originally published here: 

https://techsambad.substack.com/p/ais-growth-paradox-why-more-data

References :

https://www.businessinsider.com/openai-orion-model-scaling-law-silicon-valley-chatgpt-2024-11

https://time.com/7178328/is-ai-progress-slowing-down/

https://techcrunch.com/2024/07/17/ttt-models-might-be-the-next-frontier-in-generative-ai/

https://menlovc.com/2024-the-state-of-generative-ai-in-the-enterprise/

entry.title: Discover the SAP Defense and Security Solutions with the new SAP Solution Scout
entry.content:
What is the SAP Solution Scout and what Benefits does it Offer?

 

Defense and Security (D&S) organizations require reliable and sophisticated software solutions with advanced functions and features that allow them to manage all parts of the organization according to their specific requirements. SAP recognized these needs long ago when it created the first Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) software solution in 1972. Today, SAP is the world’s largest provider of enterprise application software and the market leading ERP software vendor for the D&S industry, offering a broad range of solutions for 26 different industries. This extensive solution portfolio can create complexity for clients and partners when trying to find the right product for their needs. That is why SAP developed the Solution Scout, an interactive journey map for SAP products and solutions. The Solution Scout, available for 28 industries, allows interested parties, such as Defense and Security customers, partner companies, consultants, and sales representatives, to explore SAP products and solutions within each industry. It provides an overview of the core product portfolio for each industry at a glance. Additionally, users can learn more about each solution area, discover individual solutions, and understand the specific value they offer. Moreover, the SAP Solution Scout enables users to create a collection of SAP solutions, even across industries, that best reflects the specific needs of their company or organization.

 

 

The Defense and Security Solution Scout

 

The SAP Defense and Security Solution Scout has recently been added to the tool. As described above, it allows clients, prospective clients, and other interested parties to explore the SAP Defense and Security core product portfolio in detail. This includes:

  • Solutions for Military Planning and Operations
  • Logistics and Order Fulfillment Solutions
  • Military Supply Chain Solutions
  • or Maintenance and Engineering Solutions

For each of these core solutions, users can find detailed information, discover all related (sub-)solutions, and create an individual collection of SAP products and solutions that best meets their requirements. They can then use the gathered information for internal discussions or to directly contact the SAP sales team or their individual customer advisor to implement the respective solutions in their organization.

 

 

Additional Products and Solutions for D&S Organizations

 

While the SAP Defense and Security Solution Scout covers the core products and solutions, SAP offers much more to its Defense and Security customers. For instance, clients can also utilize cross-industry solutions such as those for Human Capital Management, Sustainability, or Financial Management, and solutions from other industries if applicable. Moreover, SAP provides the opportunity to create cutting-edge customized software solutions tailored to specific needs and requirements. The SAP offer for the Defense and Security industry is complemented by various supplementary, best-in-class partner solutions. Some of these partner solutions are directly available in the SAP Store: Link to the SAP Store

 

 

Accessing the SAP Solution Scout

 

The new SAP Solution Scout for Defense and Security is accessible via the following link: Link to the SAP Solution Scout. Simply click on the Link to the SAP Solution Scout and select Defense & Security among the industries to access the page. If you do not have a SAP Universal ID yet, please register on the following page to access the SAP Solution Scout: Link to SAP Universal ID Account Creation.

entry.title: How to get Audit Trail Section in Print Reports in SAP Concur
entry.content:
I always look up the internet for ideas and solutions whenever I get stuck with something and finding what we are looking for can become a tedious task especially in niche technologies like Concur. And when I don't find the exact thing but later come up with a solution, I tend to make a playbook so that it might come in handy in future. But now that I have got an idea how blogging works, I feel this is even better as it reaches a wider audience and can help someone like me who is finding a solution.

So coming to the task that made me sweep the various documents and posts was a very valid ask from one of our business team members. They wanted to have the Audit Trail section come in the Print Report so that they can download it.

The business case as I understood from him was many times the admin team members who do the initial audit get blamed for Late fees, which happens when the report is not processed on time.

The Audit team find it difficult to get into the audit trail section for those reports which are in question take a screenshot of the day and time they have approved it from their to ensure it was not them who were the reason for late fees but the submission from users were found to be delayed.

This cumbersome manual process is something we can eliminate if we can have a Print Report which gives out the Audit Trail section. They can just download the PDF and send it to the respective stakeholders.

So when trying to check out the existing templates we do not have any with regards to Audit trail. And when you go to the internet and browse there is just one similar question back from 2022 which was never answered.

And then when you check the training documents from concur only the below portion is mentioned 

  • Audit Trail: Displays audit trail data, including confirmation agreement acceptance records, to the printed report. This data appears in a non-configurable preset format.

Displays audit trail data, including confirmation agreement acceptance records, to the printed report. This data appears in a non-configurable preset format.

Syntax: $auditdata_XXXX

(where XXXX is the content section name, such as $auditdata_AuditTrail)

Also to note is The printed report will create the audit trail information based on the role of the user who is printing the report. For example, a processor sees more types of information on the audit trail than a submitter. When the processor prints the report, their audit trail section will contain more details than when the submitter prints the same report.

But apart from that there is no real information about anything. So I have come up with the HTML Code that will ease up the process and also create a template which will include only the Audit Trail section.

The Actual Code:

<tr>
<td>
<table class="cStdForm"
cellspacing="0"
style="width: 100%"
cellpadding="0"
border="0"
summary="$!ui.localize('table.summary.printreportreceipt')">
<tr><td> $auditdata_AuditTrailSection </td></tr>
</table>
</td>
</tr>

Now that we have the code it is just a matter of fitting it into a template and assigning that Print report to the required policy.

Step 1:

Go to Home->Expense->Printed Reports->Print Templates

Here copy an existing template and rename it . Then open the newly copied report and in the template code make sure to place our code which is given above in a place where you desire. The position can be seen by clicking on the preview layout. Then click on save.

Make sure you are careful with all the open and close tags when you remove contents you don't need from the template. 

Step 2:

Now move to the Print Formats tab. Similar way copy an existing Format and rename it. In the Print 

template field choose the template we just created in the previous step.

and preview the layout and make sure it is as per the requirements.

Now go back to the Print Format tab and click on Modify content

In the Modify Content page you will see all the headers of the sections we have inserted in the template.

Now click on the Audit Trail and click on Modify content.

In the modify content of the Audit trail section give a suitable header and also make sure you check the Print Confirmation Agreement Audit trail records check box.

Step 3:

Now head to the policy where this Print Report is required and enable it at Policy level.

Once this is done the go to the processor, search for the report for which we need the Audit Trail to be printed out and download the same.

The final output of the PDF look like below with the Employee data and the Audit trail section.

So there it is , a way to add the Audit Trail section to the Print Template and I hope this will help anyone who are looking for a solution to this type of a requirement.

Thanks a lot everyone!

entry.title: Integration & Exception Monitoring Two-Tier ERP
entry.content:
Introduction

Integration and exception monitoring in a Two-Tier ERP system are essential to ensure seamless data flow and consistency between Headquarter and Subsidiary systems. They help maintain operational efficiency, avoid data discrepancies, and support accurate reporting across business units. Real-time exception monitoring enables quick identification and resolution of issues, preventing disruptions. Proper monitoring ensures that any deviations are swiftly addressed, ensuring smooth, unified business operations

Set up:

Let see a detailed view on how we can set up Integration & Exception Monitoring for SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Public cloud Edition and CI.

The details are also available in reference Link.

Process

Let’s deep dive into the process looking at the use case.

Case 1:  In 2EL scenario  we send the sales order to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition  from On-premise(SAP S/4HANA) system as Headquarter wants to purchase from Subsidiary.

In this case the communication is failing at Headquarter.

Case 2:  In 2EL scenario we send the sales order to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition  from On-premise system(SAP S/4HANA) as Headquarter wants to purchase from Subsidiary.

In this case the communication is failing at CI which is a middleware.

References

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/landscape-setup

https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/setup-managed-services.html

https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations.html?anchorId=section_1159650512

entry.title: ABAP Object Oriented Programming (OOP): Builder pattern Cloud Ready Version.
entry.content:
Builder Pattern Cloud Ready Implementation

All code can be found in my GitHub repository: https://github.com/IlyaPrusakou/ABAPBuilderPattern

In this repository you can find implementation of Builder Pattern in ABAP. The main point is the realization of pattern made on special version of ABAP language - ABAP for Cloud Development. So that there is 100% cloud compatibility.

Just copy and use it.

1. Introduction.

The Builder pattern is one of the classic creational design pattern. The main idea of the one is to separate process of creation of Product class (Product, Car, Car Schema and etc.) A logic for creation of Product class is handled into Builder class.

Below you can find Pattern Schemas and Implementation Details, you can skip theoretical notes and go to these sections.

2. Theoretical notes.

2.1. Types of the Builder pattern

There are 3 types of builder pattern I've differentiated. Each of them can be found correspondingly in GitHub files: src/zpru_bld1, src/zpru_bld2, src/zpru_bld3.

2.1.1. Simple Builder:

2.1.2. Builder with Director

2.1.3. Builder with Director and Interfaces

2.2. Semantical structure of pattern

All classes and interfaces can play roles of:

  1. Director
  2. Builder
  3. Product.

Regarding of Product role I would like to underline distinction of at least two type of specialization:

2.2.1. Factual specialization

Factual specialization means that all instances of product has the same class or interface, but they differ by data preserved into. The example:

As you can see there are two instances of class Car, but they are varied with qualified field model, which can be read-only field, set inside constructor.

2.2.2. Type specialization

As you can guessed that type of specialization is based on usage of not only the same class with different values of qualified fields, but also different classes or interfaces. Moreover, there is room for amplification of interfaces raised. The same example:

Regarding Builder role I would elucidate method Build. The method creates an instance of Product and set all data, preserved in Builder instance, into product instance. We can set up builder once with setters methods. All the data will be plugged in Product instance. Each invocation of Build creates new instance of Product with data from Builder. The Builder is a source of data, along with that Product is a recipient of data. Moreover, there is space for implementation of specialization of product. For instance, Builder can have multiple Build methods (buildProductA, buildProductB and etc.), returning different instance setup or different class types.

Director role is just additional stage of Product creation. When Builder is a distinct layer for creation of Products, but Director is a layer to create or setup of Builders. Hence the question of specialization rises again there. For example, we can make multiple methods (setupBuilderA, setupBuilderB and etc.) for setup of different Builders.

3. Pattern Schemas.

3.1. Simple Builder

3.2. Builder with Director.

3.3. Builder With Director and Interfaces

 

4. Implementation Details

4.1. Simple Builders

  1. Car class has private constructor an establish friendship with Car Builder class.
  2. Car class has only getters methods to receive data from.
  3. Car class has constructor taking all data to setup it as class attributes.
  4. Builder contains only setter methods to setup builder data as class attributes.
  5. Builder has build method, returning instance of Car class.
  6. Builder's build method passes all data from Builder instance into constructor of Car class.

4.2. Simple Builders Usage pattern.

 

    " Create mercedes builder
    " pass mandatory fields
    DATA(lo_mercedes_builder) = NEW zpru_cl_bld1_car_builder( iv_id    = '1'
                                                              iv_brand = 'MERCEDES'
                                                              iv_model = 'C 180 T-Model' ).
    " pass optional fields into builder
    lo_mercedes_builder->set_price('100000' )->set_multimedia( 'AVANTGARDE' )->set_equipment( 'Memory Package' )->set_assistance_system('Driving Assistance Package Plus' ).

    " construct final object of mercedes
    DATA(lo_mercedes_c_180) = lo_mercedes_builder->build( ).

 

4.3. Builder with Director

  1. 1 - 6 points for Simple Builder are true
  2. Director class has specialized methods to setup different Builders, e.g. buildMercedes, buildPorcshe and etc.
  3. Director's specialized methods has importing parameter of type of Builder class(Builder Interface) to inject Builder instance
  4. Director's methods provide distinguished setup for different Builders' instances.

4.4. Builder with Director Usage pattern

 

    DATA(lo_director) = NEW zpru_cl_bld2_car_director( ).
    DATA(lo_mercedes_builder) = NEW zpru_cl_bld2_car_builder( iv_brand = 'MERCEDES'
                                                              iv_model = 'C 180 T-Model' ).
    lo_director->build_mercedes( io_builder = lo_mercedes_builder ).

    lo_mercedes_builder->set_color( 'BLUE' ).
    lo_mercedes_builder->set_id( '1' ).
    DATA(lo_mercedes_c180_1_blue) = lo_mercedes_builder->build( ).

 

4.5. Builder With Director and Interfaces

  1. Basically, 1 - 6 points for Simple Builder are true.
  2. Create common interface for all Product class(Car class and Car Schema class).
  3. Create product specialized interfaces for Car entity and Car Schema entity.
  4. Create common interface for Builder classes(Car Builder class and Car Schema class).
  5. Create Builder specialized interfaces for Car Builder entity and Car Schema Builder entity.
  6. Rework friendship inside Car and Car Schema classes, make it points onto Builder common interface.
  7. Use type of product specialized interface as returning type of Builder's build methods.
  8. Use type of builder specialized interface as importing type of Director's build methods.

4.6. Builder With Director and Interfaces Usage pattern.

 

    DATA lo_mercedes_builder_car TYPE REF TO zpru_if_bld3_builder_car.
    DATA lo_mercedes_builder_schema TYPE REF TO zpru_if_bld3_builder_schema.
    DATA lo_director TYPE REF TO zpru_cl_bld3_car_director.

    lo_director = NEW zpru_cl_bld3_car_director( ).
    lo_mercedes_builder_car = NEW zpru_cl_bld3_car_builder( iv_brand = 'MERCEDES'
                                                            iv_model = 'C 180 T-Model' ).
    lo_director->build_mercedes_car( io_builder = lo_mercedes_builder_car ).

    lo_mercedes_builder_car->zpru_if_bld3_builder~set_id( '1' ).
    DATA(lo_mercedes_c180_car) = lo_mercedes_builder_car->build( ).

    lo_mercedes_builder_schema = NEW zpru_cl_bld3_schema_builder( iv_brand = 'MERCEDES'
                                                                  iv_model = 'C 180 T-Model' ).

    lo_director->build_mercedes_schema( io_builder = lo_mercedes_builder_schema ).
    lo_mercedes_builder_schema->zpru_if_bld3_builder~set_id( '2' ).
    DATA(lo_mercedes_c180_schema) = lo_mercedes_builder_schema->build( ).

entry.title: Material Staging in Production Supply Area-WM/PP Integration
entry.content:
Integrating warehouse and production processes through material staging at the production supply area. This integration will ensure a seamless flow of materials, reduce downtime, and improve overall productivity.

Configuration Process

Storage type for Production supply area (123-Prod. Supply Area)

SPRO -> Logistics Execution -> Warehouse Management -> Interfaces -> Define Production

Click Maintain

Click Assign (319-Mvt. Type Replenishment)

Click PP

Maintain wm request as “X-Create TR upon order release” Under Transport sub screen

Click Warehouse Number

Tick PP Interface Active

Note: We can proceed without checking this indicator also, process flow works as follows;

If checked

Prod. Order -> Transfer stock to Prod. Supply area -> TR -> TO -> Confirm TO -> Goods Issue to Prod. Order

If not checked

Prod. Order -> Goods Issue to Prod. Order -> TR -> TO -> Confirm TO

Test Scenario:

Step 1: Create finished material and components, make sure you have stock for the component material.

(Extend WM, work scheduling, MRP views for materials)

Step 2: Assign Prod. Supply area to work center using transaction CR02

Step 3: Create Control cycle using transaction LPK1

Staging Indicator 1 represents “Items picked against respective production order only”.

Fixed bin created & assigned for prod. Supply area storage type(123) i.e wm-pp-area

Step 4: Create Production Order using transaction CO01

The below component copied from the BOM,

To produce 1 PC of final product we require 25 PC of raw material (wm-pp-raw-mat)

Click on Operations

Work center operation assigned here

Click Release order and save the Order

Step 5: Stage the material with respect to production order in Prod. Supply Area using transaction LP10

Enter Requested Qty and save

Step 6: Check the Open transfer Requirements in transaction LB10

Step 7: Create and confirm TO

Step 8: Check the stock either in LS26 or LX02

As per production requirement 25 PC stagged in Prod. Supply area

Step 9: Goods Issue Against Production order using transaction MIGO

Once material document posted stock automatically consumed to the production form prod. Supply area

Step 10: Check the stock

Available stock 25 PC and 25 PC from prod. Supply area storage type got consumed against the production order.

entry.title: Flexible Item Category Determination in SAP Cloud Public - Custom Adaptions
entry.content:
Why Flexible Item Category Determination Matters!

As Sales Configuration Experts, we all know that the Item categories control how sales document items behave and thus meet different demands in the business processes.

However, the limitations of the Sales Public Cloud prevent the creation of Z-Sales Document Types. As a result, we are unable to control the determination of the appropriate item category that will control the item behavior in the sales documents. Consequently, most users are required to make manual changes when processing these documents.

Flexible Determination

Using the Flexible Item Category Determination, The system can evaluate specific business conditions and propose an item category according to your business rules instead of the Default Item Category assignment.

Example:

Your client needs to manage the behavior of business processes by selecting the appropriate Order Reason. For a certain type of orders, a down payment is required for all products. Therefore, the client will select "Down Payment" from the dropdown list. Once this selection is made, the system can propose the CBAO item category when specific business conditions outlined in a decision table are met, allowing the billing plan to be automatically enabled.

Define the following Business Rule to Your Decision Table.

 

Sales Document Type Item Category Group Item Category Usage Item Category of Higher-Level Item SD Order Reason Item Category

 

= OR= NORM<empty><empty>= 01 (Down Payment)= CBAO

Note: If the user doesn't select any order reason or any available alternatives, the system finds no logic in your decision table. The system proposes the default item category TAN.

Step-by-Step Guide to Configuration

Step 1: Create PRFplus Objects

you can create Business Rule Framework Plus (BRFplus) applications, functions, and expressions from templates.

You perform this in the configuration activity Create BRFplus Objects for Sales Document Categories.

  • Custom Adaption: ITEM_CATEGORY_DETERMINATITEION

 

  • Select Document Category: C (Orders) And Press Enter

     

A BRFplus application template is provided. The application template includes a function template and an expression template.

 

  • Press The Copy Button to Enter the PRFplus Object (PRFplus Application)  

     

     

  • Save Your Configuration
  • Navigate Simplified BRFplus Workbench app and Configure your Business Rules.

     

  • In the following Application you can define the new Business Rules to your Decision Table.
    • Find your application in the repository.
    • Expand the application node, the expression node, and then the decision table node.
    • Choose the decision table to display it.
    • Click Edit

 

 

 

  • Now you can add a row to your decision table and add values in cells as shown below.

     

  • You can now check, save and activate Your Business Rules.
     
  • Recommendation

    To prevent any inconsistencies between your business rules and the existing item category assignments in the Assign Item Categories configuration activity, choose values for the following conditions and the result by using the search help:

    • Sales document type
    • Item category group
    • Item category of higher-level item
    • Item usage
    • Item category

     Results

    The implementation is complete for the sales document category to which the activated BRFplus application, function, and expression are assigned. The flexible item category determination is enabled for sales documents that belong to the sales document category. 

Testing the Configuration

  1. Create Standard Sales Order And Choose the Order Reason defined in your Business Rule.

 

Thanks!

entry.title: File Uplaod in cloud using RAP.
entry.content:
RAP File Uploads 

In the context of SAP's Rapid Application Programming (RAP) model, file uploads enable you to incorporate file handling capabilities into your applications. This empowers users to upload various file types, such as PDFs, Excel spreadsheets, images, and more. 

Uploading Large Object and media such as Excel or Image through your application is a common business requirement and the only way to do it through a RAP application was by extending the application and using UI5 tooling to upload the file. 

With the latest SAP BTP ABAP 2208 release the RAP framework now supports OData streams. It is now possible to enable your RAP application to maintain and handle Large Objects(LOBs).This feature provides end users an option to upload external files of different file formats such as PDF, XLSX, binary file format and other types hence allowing media handling. 

In this Blog we will explore how to upload and handle Large Object such as PDF or Binary files without the need to extend the RAP application in BAS. 

Large objects are modeled by means of the following fields: 

Attachment 

Mime type 

Filename 

The field Attachment contains the LOB itself in a RAWSTRING format and is technically bound to the field Mime type and Filename using semantics annotation. 

Mimetype represents the content type of the attachment uploaded and the values for the fields Mime type and Filename are derived from the field Attachment by the RAP framework based on the maintained CDS annotations. No attachment can exist without its mime type and vice versa. 

For example, when a PDF is uploaded the Mime type field will be derived and populated with ‘APPLICATION/PDF’ 

 

Key Considerations: 

  • RAP Version: The specific approach to file uploads may vary slightly depending on the RAP version you're using. 
  • File Types: The types of files you intend to support will influence the configuration and implementation. 
  • File Size Limits: Consider setting appropriate file size limits to prevent performance issues and potential security risks. 
  • Security: Implement robust security measures to protect uploaded files, including access control and data encryption. 

General Steps: 

  1. Model the File: Define a suitable data type for the file in your RAP business object. Consider using a Large Object (LOB) data type to accommodate large files. 
  2. Create the Upload Service: Expose a service that allows clients to upload files. Implement the necessary logic to handle the uploaded file, such as saving it to a specific location or processing its content. 
  3. Client-Side Implementation: Create a user interface element (e.g., a file input field) to allow users to select files. Use appropriate HTTP methods (e.g., POST) to send the file to the server. Handle the server's response to indicate success or failure of the upload. 

Specific Approaches: 

  • OData Streams:  
  • Leverage OData streams to directly handle file uploads within your RAP application. 
  • This approach is well-suited for modern RAP versions and provides a streamlined way to manage file uploads. 

Business scenario. 

In the Sales and Distribution (SD) module, companies often receive bulk sales orders from customers in the form of Excel or CSV files, especially in industries like wholesale or distribution. Manually entering these orders into SAP is time-consuming and prone to errors.   

Database Tables.

 

 

@EndUserText.label : 'material header data' 

@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE 

@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT 

@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A 

@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED 

define table znp_dt_mara1 {

mtart : mtart; 

mbrsh : mbrsh; 

@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure : 'znp_t_mara.gewei'

@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure : 'znp_t_mara.gewei' 

mweight : brgew;

attachement : znp_de_media; 

filename : abap.char(128); 

mimetype : abap.char(128); 

created_by : abp_creation_user; 

created_at : abp_creation_tstmpl; 

last_changed_by : abp_locinst_lastchange_user; 

last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl; 

 

}

mweight as Mweight, 

materialstatus as Materialstatus, 

lvorm as Lvorm, 

@Semantics.largeObject : { mimeType: 'Mimetype',

@Semantics.mimeType: true 

mimetype as Mimetype,

created_by as CreatedBy, 

@Semantics.systemDateTime.createdAt: true 

created_at as CreatedAt, 

@Semantics.user.localInstanceLastChangedBy: true

}

entry.title: Storage Unit Management with Identification and Pick Points in WM
entry.content:
Storage Unit Management in SAP WM allows for the efficient handling of storage units within a warehouse. A storage unit can be any container or pallet that holds materials.

Identification Points in SAP WM

Identification points are critical for tracking and managing storage units. These points are strategically placed within the warehouse to ensure that each storage unit is correctly identified and recorded.

Pick Points in SAP WM

Pick points are designated areas within the warehouse where materials are collected for order fulfillment. Effective management of pick points can significantly enhance the picking process.

Let's consider an example scenario for ID Point;

ID Point Storage Type: 122 – Identification Point

Main Storage Type: 118 – BC Contact Materials

If material is coming to main destination Storage type, then first it must come to ID Point Storage type and then it must move from ID point storage type to Main Storage type.

1. Storage type Configuration Path:

IMG > Logistics Execution > Warehouse Management > Master Data > Define Storage Type

Select ID Point Storage Type and navigate to details

Stock Removal Strategy is not required for ID point storage type

Select Main Storage Type and navigate to details

 

Maintain Storage type as 118 for Put away and stock Removal operations (with or without storage type Indicator)

2. Storage type Bin search Configuration Path:

IMG > Logistics Execution > Warehouse Management > Strategies > Activate Storage Bin Type Search

Test Case

Step 1: Create a PO and do the GRN

Step 2: Check the stock before doing GRN

Step 3: Create TO using LT06 and transfer the stock using Foreground option.

Step 4: Check stock in LS26, stock updated in ID point Storage Type.

Uploaded stock 50,000 EA (500 BOX)

Total Stock: 50,350 EA

Step 5: Create Transfer order to move the stock from ID point to Main storage type using LT09 Transaction.

Tick Confirm check box and click on Create Transfer order

Once you click on Create TO, Destination Storage Bin details automatically fetched

Step 6: Check the stock in LS26 Transaction

Let's consider an example scenario for Pick Point;

Pick Point Storage Type: 122 – Pick Point Point

Main Storage Type: 118 – BC Contact Materials

Once Transfer confirmed and stock removed from the SU, remaining stock wil automatically shown in 122 storage tye (Pick point), This leftover stock will be moved back to original main store by creating TO using LT09.

Step 1: Check Full stock removal check box in storage type master data screen

Step 2: Check the stock in LS26

In our case 1BAG contains 100 EA.

Total stock 50,000 EA

Step 3: Goods Issue posted for 150 EA using MIGO Transaction

Step 4: Create Transfer order

Step 5: Confirm Transfer order item using LT11 Transaction

If stock removal happens from multiple storage units.

  1. Enter the number of first storage unit that you want to remove completely from storage
  2. Enter the number of second storage units that you want to completely remove from the stock.

Once you have confirmed the last TO item for picking, the system posts the SU with the remaining quantity of 49,850 EA to the pick point (122 Str. type). The SU is therefore at the pick point, both physically and according to the system.

Step 6: Check the stock

Remaining stock will automatically shown in storage type 122 (Pick point str. Type)

To move this stock back to main storage type, create TO in LT09 Transaction

Step 7: Create TO using LT09 Transaction.

As per storage bin type search strategy system will picks the storage bin automatically by clicking the create Transfer Order and confirm check box.

Step 8: Confirm the TO

Step 9: Check the stock in LS26 Transaction

entry.title: Version Management in Purchase Order(PO)
entry.content:
Version Management in SAP allows users to create and manage different versions of a purchase order. Each version captures the state of the PO at a specific point in time, enabling users to track changes and maintain a history of modifications. This is particularly useful in complex procurement processes where multiple changes may occur before finalizing a PO.

Step 1: Activate Version Management

To use version management, it must first be activated in the SAP system. This can be done through the following steps:

  • Go to SPRO.
  • Navigate to Materials Management > Purchasing > Purchase Order > Version Management.
  • Activate version management for the relevant document types.

Step 2: Configure Version Management

Once activated, configure the settings to suit your organization's needs:

  • Define the version number increment.
  • Set up the conditions under which a new version is created (e.g., changes in quantity, price, delivery date).
  • Specify the fields that should trigger version creation.

Step 3: Create and Manage Versions

When changes are made to a PO, a new version is created automatically based on the configured settings. Users can view and manage these versions through the PO history:

  • Access the PO in the SAP system.
  • Navigate to the Version Overview tab to see all versions.
  • Compare different versions to understand the changes made.

Process Steps:

1.Set Up Version Management for External Purchasing Document

SPRO >Materials Management > Purchasing > Version Management > Set Up Version Management for External Purchasing Documents.

Select the document type and navigate to Details option

In control data, check the version active and version 0 OK and in field selection based on your business needs chose whether the fields are optional or mandatory entries.

2. Define Reasons for Change

SPRO > Materials Management > Purchasing > Version Management > Define Reasons for Change

Here you can maintain the different reasons for changinf the purchase order

3. Set Up Change Displays

SPRO > Materials Management > Purchasing > Version Management > Set Up Change Displays

Enter fields for document type and check for version as active

4. Create Purchase Order

Version 0 will be created automatically once after the PO creation

5. Change the PO

Quantity change from 3 to 5

Since we chosen this fields as required fields user must enter the data to save the PO

Once saved new version will be created in the Version tab in PO header

Price changed from 300 EUR to 500 EUR

Version 2 generated for the new changes

entry.title: Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Business Context

The process for advanced intercompany stock transfer allows you to transfer physical stock from one plant to another plant in the same system and the plants are owned by different companies.

A new functionality is introduced in SAP S/4HANA OP2022, OP2023, and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, for intercompany stock transport. While the classic intercompany stock transport order remains available, the advanced intercompany stock transport order offers several additional benefits:

  • Track Sales KPIs
  • Reuse Valuated Stock in Transit (VSIT) for transfer of control
  • Utilize VSIT for both the delivering and receiving companies
  • Support the matching principle for financial postings, ensuring simultaneous recognition of Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) and revenue, thereby achieving compliance with IFRS 15.

Process:

The process starts with the creation of a Stock Transport Order (STO). The supplier in the purchase order is a business partner representing a plant from a different company code. The purchase order is created using the document type NBIC (Intercompany Stock Transfer STO).

Once the purchase order is processed, a Sales Order is immediately created with the document type CBIC (Intercompany Order).

SAP provides an app called “Monitor Value Chains”, which displays the entire document flow.

Next, an outbound delivery is created against the STO using transaction VL10B, followed by Post Goods Issue (PGI) using transaction VL02N. Upon PGI, two material documents are automatically created in the background:

  1. A material document with movement type 681 (Stock Transfer to Issuing Valuated Cross-Company-Code Stock in Transit (CST)).
  2. Another material document with movement type 685 (Stock Transfer from Issuing Valuated CST).

Simultaneously, another material document is created in the receiving plant with movement type 107 (Goods Receipt in Valuated Goods Receipt Blocked Stock). This material document is generated by a background job based on the planned execution date.

Once the material reaches the receiving plant, the receiving team posts the Goods Receipt using transaction VL02N, resulting in a material document with movement type 109 (Goods Receipt from Valuated Goods Receipt Blocked Stock).

The receiving company then creates an invoice using transaction VF01 against the outbound delivery. Subsequently, an invoice is automatically created in the supplying company, and a supplier invoice is posted in the receiving plant (via an automatic MIRO process).

Document Flow:

The entire document flow is visible in the “Monitor Value Chains” app.

You can monitor related logs using transaction SLG1 with the following parameters:

  • Object: VCM
  • Subobject: *

Configuration

  1. Create BP for suppling plant and receiving plant.
  2. Extend supplier role for supplying plant and customer role for receiving plant.
  3. Use standard document type – NBIC for creating STO.

4. Use delivery type – NCC3 for outbound delivery and – NCC3 for delivery item category.

5. Use standard schedule lien category – N3

6. Configure Delivery Type & Availability Check Procedure by Plant.

7. Use Standard billing type – IV2S for the cancellation of the invoice and IV2 for intercompany invoice and IV3 for intercompany proforma invoice

8. Use sales order type – CBIC for the intercompany sales order.

9. Define schedule line category – CI for the intercompany sales order

10. Define jobs to Execute Scheduled Process Steps

IMG>ABAP Platform>Application Server>System Administration>Activation of Scope-Dependent Background Job Definitions

11. Tcode : SJOBREPO. Customize the job

12. Activate event type linkage

SPRO -> ABAP Platform -> SAP Gateway Service Enablement → Backend OData Channel → Backend Event Publisher Administration → Event Type Linkages.

13. Assign Value Chain Category to Sales Document Type

SPRO -> Sales and Distribution -> Sales → Sales Documents → Integration with Value Chain Monitoring → Assign Value Chain Category to Sales Document Type

14. Assign Value Chain Category to Item Category.

SPRO -> Sales and Distribution -> Sales → Sales Documents → Integration with Value Chain Monitoring → Assign Value Chain Category to Item Category.

15. Assign Document Type and Item Category to Value Chain.

SPRO -> Sales and Distribution -> Sales → Sales Documents → Integration with Value Chain Monitoring → Assign Document Type and Item Category to Value Chain.

 

16. Enable Value Chain Monitoring for Purchase Orders.

SPRO -> Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Advanced Intercompany Processes → Enable Value Chain Monitoring for Purchase Orders

17. Assign tax Codes

SPRO -> Materials Management → Logistics Invoice Verification → Advanced Intercompany Processes → Assign Tax Codes for the Transfer of Supplier Invoices.

18. Configure Program Parameter Configuration IDs

SPRO -> Materials Management → Logistics Invoice Verification → Advanced Intercompany Processes → Configure Program Parameters

19. Assign Program Parameter

SPRO -> Materials Management → Logistics Invoice Verification → Advanced Intercompany Processes → Assign Program Parameter

20. Configure Posting Date for Advanced Intercompany Invoices

SPRO -> Materials Management → Logistics Invoice Verification → Advanced Intercompany Processes → Configure Posting Date for Advanced Intercompany Invoices

Thanks,

Ranadeep Basak

entry.title: Detailed Scheduling Missing for Planned Orders After MRP Run - SAP Upgrade
entry.content:
Hello all,

We recently upgraded our SAP system to the latest version, retaining all master data and customizations as they were before. However, I’m encountering an issue with detailed scheduling for planned orders during MRP runs:

  • Issue Description:
    When MRP creates a planned order, the capacity requirements are not scheduled, and the Detailed Scheduling tab is missing in the planned order. Specifically:

    • Entries in KBKO and KBED tables (capacity requirements) are not created.
    • The BEDID (Capacity Requirement ID) is not passed to the PLAF table.

    However, if I manually open the planned order created by MRP and click the Scheduling button:

    • Capacity requirements are scheduled.
    • Entries in KBKO and KBED are created.
    • The BEDID is updated in the PLAF table.

  • Additional Context:

    1. This issue only occurs in the updated system; it was working fine before the upgrade - MRP did generate scheduling and the table entries created.
    2. It still works correctly in our quality system, which is on the previous SAP version.
    3. I have tested MRP via MD01N and SAPAPO Product View, but detailed scheduling is not triggered in either case.

  • Troubleshooting Attempts:

    • Debugged PPH_MRP_START, but couldn’t find the root cause.
    • Verified that all customizing and master data are unchanged from the previous version.

  • Question:
    Could you help me understand:

    1. Which ABAP program, function module, or method generates the capacity requirements entries in KBKO and KBED during MRP run?
    2. How can I debug this process further to identify what’s not being triggered after the upgrade?

Any insights or suggestions to debug the issue backward would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance for your help.

SCM APO Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling (PP/DS)

entry.title: SAP Field Service Management 2411 - Available Now!
entry.content:
SAP Field Service Management 2411 builds on our strategic investment in generative AI for field service organizations, now with extended multilingual AI support. In addition, we introduced several new features designed to simplify user workflows, strengthen integration, and increase functionality. In this blog, you can find an overview of the most notable features, along with a demo video showcasing key highlights. For a complete list of all the features in the 2411 release, click here. In this blog, I will highlight the key features that will help enhance how you manage field services, including:

  • Generative AI: Multilingual support (French and German) for the Activity Summary, Equipment Insights, and Natural Language Filtering.
  • User Experience enhancements: New Morning Horizon theme, Business Partner enhancements, manage work time in the Time & Material Journal, extended sort and filter capabilities, extended customization capabilities, and more.
  • Service Map: Visualize activity details on hover, and a simplified view of travel time/distance.
  • Integration: Enhanced SAP S/4HANA Service integration, New Fiori App for viewing SAP FSM activities, Production Order integration, and item-based accounting for an unplanned service.
  • AI-based scheduling improvements: Simulate the effect of adjustments in auto-scheduling policies, and recalculate travel time for bookings.

Note: The Activity Summary, Equipment Insights, and Natural Language Filtering features require a customer to be licensed for SAP AI Units. For more information about licensing, please contact your SAP Account Executive. 

Generative AI

SAP Field Service Management Generative AI services are now available in French and German, enabling more users to access Gen AI features in their native language. In addition to simplifying workflows, generative AI in SAP Field Service Management makes it easier to unlock and benefit from valuable insights that were previously difficult to access. These enhanced workflows and actionable insights provide substantial value to field service organizations. According to SAP customer benchmarking, by utilizing these features, field service organizations can achieve (on average):

  • ⬆️ 12.5% increase in dispatcher productivity.
  • ⬆️ 5% improvement in first-time fix rates.
  • ⬇️ 15% reduction in time to review and plan service requests.

Take a look at the demo video below showcasing our AI highlights in 2411:


AI Assisted Services - Now Available in German and French

AI Assisted Services (German 🇩🇪)

AI Services in German - Intelligent Filtering and Activity Summary

 
AI Assisted Services (French 🇫🇷) 

AI Services in French - Intelligent Filtering and Activity Summary
➡️ For more information on AI services such as Intelligent Filtering, Equipment Insights, and the Activity Summary, please refer to the 2408 Release Blog. These translations are made possible using SAP Translation Hub.

AI-based Scheduling Improvements:

Simulate the Effect of Adjustments in Custom Auto-scheduling Policies

The new Policy Simulation feature allows users to test scheduling policy changes in a controlled environment, ensuring no impact on live schedules. This enhancement allows users to configure and evaluate adjustments, such as prioritizing skills levels or enforcing objectives, in a secure environment. By simulating outcomes, organizations can safely refine strategies, and ensure alignment with evolving operating conditions. Learn more about this innovation here.

Recalculate Travel Time for Bookings

Thanks to feedback from our Customer Influence Program, accurate travel times are now easier to achieve via automatic travel time recalculation. Travel times are updated automatically when planning or replanning activities, ensuring dispatchers always have precise data. This enhancement helps reduce manual adjustments, minimizes dispatcher confusion, and improves overall scheduling accuracy. Learn more about this feature here.


User Experience & Usability Enhancements

SAP Field Service Management 2411 also features several key user experience and usability highlights, which are summarized below and in the demo video:


Morning Horizon Theme

The new Morning Horizon theme in SAP Field Service Management introduces a fresh, modern design, providing users with a consistent and visually appealing interface. This standard theme enhances the user experience with improved aesthetics, accessibility, and a cohesive look and feel across all SAP applications. Learn more here.

 

SAP Feedback Survey

This provides an easy way to contribute feedback on product functionality, helping SAP Field Service Management refine and enhance its offerings based on real-world usage and customer needs. Learn more about participating here.


Dispatching Board

Configurable Header Information in the Modal UI (preview)

The new header configuration in SAP Field Service Management allows organizations to customize the key details displayed in the Modal UI for Service Calls and Activities via screen configurations. This enhancement makes it easier to display critical information by tailoring screens to meet your company’s unique requirements. Learn more about this feature here.



Manage Approvals for Worktime in the Time and Material Journal

The enhanced work time management capabilities in SAP Field Service Management improve tracking and approval of time entries through the Time and Material (T&M) Journal. This update harmonizes processes in the T&M Journal and improves workflows for work time approvals. Learn more about this feature here.

 
Option to Display the Activity Cancellation Reason 

Users can now benefit from increased transparency for cancelled activities in SAP Field Service Management, with clear visibility into the reasons for activity cancellations. For more information, visit the product documentation.


Service Call Extension Tab in the Modal UI

Extensions in SAP Field Service Management now provide greater flexibility for placement in desired outlets. The new Service Call extension tab in the Modal UI allows users to position extensions in more key locations, ensuring they are easily accessible and aligned with operational priorities. For more details, click here.

Sample Extension

 
Option to Save Column Layouts in all List Views (Preview)

The 2411 release provides enhanced flexibility for customizing column settings and layouts of the activity list. Users can easily configure visible fields and group data to align with specific operational needs and tactical scheduling scenarios, creating a more intuitive user experience with faster access to relevant data. For further info, see here.

 
Enhancements for List Views

This release introduces improved filtering capabilities, offering enhanced options for managing list views. Users can now easily filter by key information when working with a list view in the planning list, making this workflow faster and more efficient. More information, here.


Service Map

Quick Access to Activity Details

Dispatchers can now view essential activity information from the Service Map by simply hovering over an activity, reducing the number of clicks. This popup allows dispatchers to instantly view key details, and can be easily configured to display the companies specific requirements. For more details, please see here.


Service Map – Close/Dock Time-Distance Popup Box

When using the Service Map, dispatchers now can easily view detailed travel information directly on the Service Map with a simple pop-up box. This pop-up displays both travel time and distance, and can be opened, moved, and closed as needed, allowing dispatchers to quickly access and manage information without cluttering the screen. More details here.


Integration Improvements

New Fiori App for Viewing SAP Field Service Management Activities

The new SAP S/4HANA Fiori App for viewing SAP FSM activities provides a simplified and intuitive way to access activity details from SAP Field Service Management. With an organized data presentation, users can quickly navigate through activities and view critical information such as status, technician assignments, and partner data, directly from SAP S/4HANA. Get the full details here.


Cancellation of Activity from SAP S/4HANA

In SAP S/4HANA, SAP FSM activities can now be cancelled with detailed reasons, while seamlessly integrated into SAP Field Service Management. This ensures consistent data and improved transparency across both systems. For more details, refer to the help documentation.


Item-based Accounting for Unplanned Service

Item-based accounting and revenue recognition in SAP S/4HANA Service now integrates with SAP Field Service Management to support unplanned services. This enhancement ensures that revenue is accurately tracked and recognized based on actual service execution, bridging financial, profitability, and operational data for improved transparency and compliance. See the support documentation here.


Integration of Production Orders as Service Calls

Production Order integration with SAP Field Service Management enhances the connection between SAP Field Service Management and SAP S/4HANA or SAP ECC. This integration enables scheduling, execution, and confirmation of production order operations, with support for time effort, materials management, and more. More information here.

Final Thoughts

SAP Field Service Management 2411 enhances the field service value chain with multilingual generative AI, streamlined workflows, and improved integration capabilities. We invite you to explore these new capabilities, share your feedback, and help us shape the future of SAP Field Service Management by submitting your influence requests here. Stay tuned for the 2502 release, where even more innovations are on the way!

Get in touch!

Thoughts or questions? Feel free to share any feedback in a comment. It would also be great to hear directly from you which AI innovations you think could be the most impactful for future releases. For related posts and more information, please join our SAP Field Service Management Community page and explore our SAP Field Service Management Website. You can also watch the SAP Field Service Management 2411 Release Demo here, and I encourage you to register for our upcoming webinars for the most up to date information by registering here.

entry.title: Re: How to check change logs for SSCUI(Configuration Acti...
entry.content:
All changes done during the evaluation or implementation project, including changes in activities, milestones, and deployment target are tracked in the Change Logs view. Users with the role SAP_CBC_CONSUMPTION_AUDITOR have access to the Change Logs view.

Also to check the change logs for an SSC UI (Configuration activity) it's not possible through the change logs tab in CBC.

To find change logs for an SSC UI check out the following KBA - 3295974 - How to check the change logs in CBC - SAP for Me

entry.title: Unable to connect to service WebiPlugin.WebiSession from server AP01 via CMS osca:iiop:
entry.content:
We often encounter Webi report failures with the following errors:

  • "Unable to connect to service WebiPlugin.WebiSession from server AP01 via CMS osca:iiop:... "
  • "An internal error occurred while calling the 'processDPCommandsEx' API. (Error: ERR_WIS_30270)"

Occasionally, the reports will succeed, so this issue appears to be intermittent.

Not sure if this is bug or network issue. But in the event log, there is time drift warning, could this be the root cause?

Warning- The time service detected a time difference of greater than 5000 milliseconds for 900 seconds. The time difference might be caused by synchronization with low-accuracy time sources or by suboptimal network conditions. The time service is no longer synchronized and cannot provide the time to other clients or update the system clock. When a valid time stamp is received from a time service provider, the time service will correct itself.

Kindly advise.

No error found on the DB side. App and DB server OS - windows server, DB - MSSQL

entry.title: SAP ALM Roadshow LAC 2024 Wrap-up
entry.content:
We're thrilled to announce the resounding success of the SAP ALM Roadshow 2024 that recently took place in São Paulo, Brazil, and Buenos Aires, Argentina. These events were a fantastic opportunity for enterprises to dive into the latest in Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) with SAP's experts, fellow industry leaders, and collaborative representatives from various SAP Lines of Business (LoBs).

Below are some topics that we covered:

  • SAP Cloud ALM for RISE with SAP and Clean Core support strategies
  • Implementing Intelligent Enterprise with SAP Cloud ALM
  • Automating Testing with Tricentis
  • Optimizing operations combining AI and SAP Cloud ALM
  • Leveraging SAP Focused Run for Advanced Monitoring Capabilities
  • Transition from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM
  • The latest information about Change Management in SAP Cloud ALM

Key Highlights:

  1. Innovative Workshops: Attendees participated in dynamic and interactive workshops that showcased the latest SAP ALM tools and methodologies tailored for the Latin American market.
  2. Expert Insights: SAP professionals shared invaluable insights into streamlining software development processes, enhancing collaboration, and gaining knowledge on latest SAP Cloud ALM updates.
  3. Regional Case Studies: Participants heard from local success stories where companies effectively implemented SAP ALM solutions to drive business growth and efficiency.
  4. Tech Demos: Live demonstrations of advanced SAP Cloud ALM features and related solutions that can propel your organization forward.
  5. Networking Sessions: Valuable networking opportunities allowed attendees to connect with peers, share experiences, and build professional relationships.
  6. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Representatives from other SAP LoBs, such as Cloud ERP, RISE with SAP, and BTP, contributed to the discussions, providing a holistic view of SAP’s ecosystem and how it integrates with SAP Cloud ALM.

Event Participation:

  • São Paulo, Brazil: November 11, 2024, witnessed over 30 different participants, from more than 10 different customers with discussions and collaborations enriching the experience. 
  • Buenos Aires, Argentina: November 19, 2024, also saw around 29 enthusiastic participants, who engaged deeply with the sessions and content provided.

Looking Ahead: The feedback we’ve received from São Paulo and Buenos Aires has been overwhelmingly positive, reinforcing the importance of regional engagements to address specific market needs. We are excited about the ongoing conversation and collaboration within the Latin American tech ecosystem.

Conclusion: The SAP ALM Roadshow 2024 has set a new standard for innovation and collaboration in Latin America. We thank all the participants from both events, as well as our collaborative partners from Cloud ERP, RISE with SAP, and BTP, for helping make this a success. We look forward to building on this success together!!

 

 SAP Cloud ALM

entry.title: “ABAP AI is our Shared Treasure” - ABAP AI is on its way.
entry.content:
It’s time to talk about the announced ABAP AI capabilities, planned to be delivered with the 2502 release and beyond. ABAP AI is important to all SAP business software developers. It is our shared treasure that we hold in our hands. Let’s take a moment to elaborate a little while on this statement.  

ABAP AI is powerful

The new Joule copilot integrated into ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse, offers a full-fledged copilot experience and by that revolutionizes the way ABAP developers work. It provides AI support during the development process, significantly accelerating the creation and testing of custom ABAP applications. These groundbreaking developments have already been revealed and demonstrated at SAP TechEd 2024.

Imagine someone sitting next to you and guiding you, to write very good ABAP code. Not too bad, you might say. Now imagine that person complementing you and even starting to write code or to do unit tests for you. You can even discuss with this person and have them explain code you don’t understand.

Let's take a closer look at some of Joule's ABAP developer capabilities.

Joule for ABAP Code Prediction (Ghost Texting)

This feature helps you code. As you start typing your code, Ghost Text uses AI to propose the next lines of code. The difference between classic code completion already available in der development tools and this new feature is that ABAP AI actually understands the context and makes intelligent suggestions that are aligned with your coding objective. This is made possible by GenAI and SAPs trained ABAP model, which is designed to understand and predict ABAP coding patterns. This allows you to spend even more time solving complex problems and innovating.

ABAP AI Unit Agent

The ABAP AI Unit Agent is designed to assist you generate unit tests for an ABAP class. This ensures that your code is thoroughly tested. Traditionally, you would have to write unit tests manually, which is time-consuming and likely to be error-prone. With the ABAP AI Unit Agent, you can automate this process: You run the unit test for the ABAP class and check the code coverage option. The ABAP Unit Test analyzes the code coverage and identifies the methods that are not adequately covered. The ABAP AI Unit Agent adds to this the generation of unit tests.

ABAP and Core Data Services Code Explain

This feature makes ABAP, including ABAP Core Data Services (CDS), easier for developers to understand. When you select an ABAP class, CDS view, function module, a method or a code snippet, ABAP & CDS Explain analyzes the code and generates a human-readable explanation. This explanation includes a high-level overview of what the code does, as well as detailed explanations of each component. It is designed to understand ABAP coding patterns and conventions, so that it can provide accurate and useful explanations. By providing clear explanations of complex function modules, it helps developers improve their understanding of ABAP coding patterns, and best practices.

ABAP AI is attractive
SAPs ABAP AI SDK, powered by Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM ), serves as an "AI toolbox" for ABAP developers. It allows  you to incorporate AI capabilities to your custom ABAP applications. The ABAP AI SDK integrates seamlessly with the SAP BTP GenAI Hub, enabling you to leverage Large Language Models (LLMs) and infuse AI capabilities into your custom applications. This SDK is also used internally for all ABAP-based AI solutions.

With the ABAP AI SDK, you can easily access and use the models and features provided by the Hub by calling released ABAP classes and interfaces directly in your application code. The ABAP AI SDK takes care of the rest, executing prompts and handling the complexities of integrating AI capabilities into ABAP. This seamless integration significantly simplifies the process of incorporating AI into business applications. If that is not attractive, what is?

ABAP AI is a treasure

Even more powerful than the ABAP AI developer use cases mentioned above is the ABAP custom code migration use case. Our shared goal is to help our customers and partners to transform their custom code and move into SAP’s cloud universe.

ABAP AI for Custom Code Migration will be a game changer in achieving this goal.

We will help you transform your custom code by accelerating the transformation of custom code. Whether it's the mandatory transformation of ABAP code when migrating from ECC to SAP S/4HANA or the subsequent transformation of ABAP code to ABAP Cloud and a Clean Core.

ABAP AI support is provided for the migration of from the classic business suite to SAP S/4HANA and to ABAP Cloud and a Clean Core. This will include explanations of the mandatory simplification items to be considered during your SAP S/4HANA transformation, as well as code suggestions for refactoring your classic ABAP code towards ABAP Cloud. The corresponding AI features will be integrated in the Custom Code Migration App as well as in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse.

Let us summarize: ABAP AI is powerful. ABAP AI is attractive. ABAP AI is a treasure.

 

Have a look at the ABAP AI roadmap for more information:
https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cross-product/roadmap-info/genai

entry.title: Re: SAP integration suite data in excel sheet
entry.content:

And if your question includes the word "transfer order" - do NOT tag the question with Extended Warehouse Management!

entry.title: Integration architecture guide: Updated version available now
entry.content:
I am happy to share the news that an updated version of the integration architecture guide has been released! Based on SAP Business Technology Platform offerings available today, this document provides you with detailed guidance on how to evolve your existing integration architecture. As before the integration guide is structured along the concepts of the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology, such as integration styles and integration use case patterns.

This blog post gives you an overview about the most important news delivered with this version of the guide.

Process Integration

This chapter has been updated to include recommendations regarding the latest capabilities of SAP Integration Suite such as the new Edge Integration Cell option, Data Space Integration and Advanced Event Mesh.

You can also find consolidated information about how to elevate toward SAP Integration Suite – be it from SAP Process Integration/Orchestration or from SAP API Management and/or SAP Cloud Integration on SAP BTP Neo cloud platform. In the first case, Edge Integration Cell plays an important role: Deployed in customer-controlled environments, it allows you to run your integration scenarios locally within your own environments, without going through the public internet. Migrated existing integrations flows can be redeployed to and run by the Edge Integration Cell.

 

Scenarios using Edge Integration Cell

Event-Driven Integration

Event-driven architecture (EDA) is a well-known approach for designing and building decoupled, hyper-connected software systems and it’s rap­idly gaining popularity in the world of enterprise integration due to its reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness. This chapter describes the role and interplay of SAP EDA solutions such as SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, SAP Cloud Application Event Hub, further capabilities of SAP Integration Suite and technologies for event-enabling SAP applications.

Event-Driven Architecture in the Enterprise

Data Integration

The guide also describes the role of SAP Datasphere with regards to data integration options. SAP Datasphere enables a business data fabric architecture and supports integration use case pattern like data ingestion, virtual data access, and data transformation and orchestration.

Overview of SAP Datasphere

User Integration

There is also a major overhaul of this chapter to provide recommendations regarding the latest SAP solutions which enable a central entry point for users to business solutions. These are SAP Start, SAP Mobile Start, SAP Build Work Zone (standard and advanced edition) and SAP Task Center.

Cross-solutions entry point on SAP Business Technology Platform

Furthermore, the technology recommendation for the chatbot integration use case pattern of the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology reflects now the latest innovations by introducing Joule: Joule is a generative AI-based assistant embedded in SAP’s cloud enterprise portfolio that provides proactive, contextualized insights from various data sources.

New Content Format and Access Point

In addition to updating the content of the integration architecture guide we also changed its format: The guide is now available as documentation at SAP Help Portal and comes with benefits such as easier navigation and search, option to bookmark, subscribe and to provide feedback to the content.

The integration architecture guide is an integral part of SAP BTP Guidance Framework which is the central access point for architects, developers and administrators to build and run enterprise-grade solutions on SAP BTP.

Integration architecture guide at SAP BTP Guidance Framework

The integration guide belongs to the decision guides of the framework and is interconnected with other assets of SAP BTP Guidance Framework such as the documentation of the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology and SAP BTP reference architectures (for integration).

Enjoy reading the new guide! It would also be great to get your feedback, for instance which integration topics should be addressed with the next update. If you have any suggestions feel free to leave them as comments to this blog post. Happy integrating 😊
